bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility while making a harmless accommodation where possible. The opponent’s opening bid gives me only moderate utility and mainly concedes on issueD and issueE, so I keep my high-weight preferred values while matching their issueD:valueB since it is equally good for me as valueA.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early (t=0.02) and the opponent has simply repeated the same low-value offer to me, worth about 0.44. Their cooperative messages are not backed by concession behavior, so I will hold firm while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid by switching issueD to an equally preferred value.

I am targeting maximum utility because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession; their latest offer is actually worse for me than their earlier ones. Their messages are cooperative but unsupported by the offer history, so I will hold firm on my high-value issues while avoiding an exact repeat of my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting near-maximum utility because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession; their offers remain low for me and even worsened on issueD. Their messages are cooperative/emotional, but the actual bid history shows they are holding firm on several high-importance issues, so I will maintain my strongest positions while varying issueD from my last bid since valueA and valueB are equally good for me there.

At this early stage (t=0.08), I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than conceding on high-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and show little meaningful concession, aside from movement on issueD where both valueA and valueB are equally best for me, so I can vary issueD without cost while holding firm elsewhere.

I am targeting maximum utility at this early stage (t=0.10), since there is still ample time and the opponent has only made very limited concessions. Their offers remain low for me on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, while their claimed cooperation is not matched by meaningful movement, so I will hold firm on my high-value issues and vary issueD, where valueA and valueB are equally good for me.

I am targeting my maximum utility because it is still very early at t=0.12, so there is little need to concede yet. The opponent has made a useful concession on issueA and varies issueD, but remains firm on low-value choices for issueB, issueC, and issueF; their emotional messages do not change the numeric assessment. To avoid repeating my exact previous bid while staying at full utility, I switch issueD to valueB, which is equally best for me.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession on a lower-weight issue. The opponent’s offers have improved for me mainly on issueA and keep signaling they want valueC on issueB/issueC/issueF, so I am holding firm on my high-weight issues and conceding slightly on issueF rather than repeating my last exact bid.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession on the lower-weight issueF. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF, with some movement on issueA and issueD, so I will keep my high-weight priorities while varying issueD, which is equally good for me at valueA or valueB.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while keeping a small concession on issueF and choosing issueD=valueB, which costs me nothing and appears often in the opponent’s bids. The opponent has conceded somewhat on issueA and recently issueC, but their offers still leave issueB and issueF at my worst values, so I am not ready to make larger concessions yet. Their messages emphasize cooperation and urgency, but the actual bid history is the reliable signal.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on the low-weight issueF. The opponent has consistently pushed issueB and issueC to valueC, which is costly for me, while already offering my preferred values on issueA, issueD, and issueE, so I hold firm on the high-weight issues and align issueD with their latest offer.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.888: I keep my high-value positions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE, while conceding moderately on issueC and issueF. At t=0.22 it is still early enough not to accept the opponent’s low-utility pattern, but their recent movement on issueC to valueB suggests that issueC may be a useful tradeoff area. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional cheap talk, so I rely more on the actual offers, which still remain far below my preferred range.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.89 because it is early-mid negotiation and there is no need to concede down to the opponent’s low-utility packages yet. The opponent’s valid offers show some movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD, while they remain firm on issueB and issueF; I will keep my high-weight issueB at my best value, accept their recent movement toward issueC=valueB, and use issueD=valueA since it is equally good for me and appeared in their latest offer.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 because we are still early at t=0.26, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA/issueC/issueD/issueE, but they remain fixed on low values for issueB and issueF; their messages are cooperative/emotional but I rely mainly on the offer pattern. I concede slightly on issueB and issueF while holding my strongest positions on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting about 0.89 utility: a meaningful concession from my top bids, but still strong because it is only early-mid negotiation at t=0.28. The opponent’s offers show real movement on issueA, issueC, issueD, and consistently give issueE, while holding firm on issueB and issueF; their emotional messages are noted but I rely mainly on the bid pattern. I therefore concede on issueC and issueF while holding my most important gains on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around 0.84, since t=0.30 is still early-mid negotiation and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s bids show some movement on issueA, issueB, and issueC, while they consistently keep issueE at valueA and issueF at valueC, so I am conceding on issueF and offering issueC=valueB while holding firm on high-value issues A, B, and D. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional appeals, so I am relying on the actual offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a utility around 0.84, which is a reasonable concession level at t=0.32 while still protecting my high-weight issues. The opponent has consistently valued issueF=valueC and issueE=valueA, and their latest offer also includes issueB=valueB and issueD=valueA, so I keep those compatible where they are low-cost or beneficial to me while holding firm on issueA and only partially conceding issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 by holding firm on the high-weight issues issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD while continuing to concede issueF, which the opponent has consistently kept at valueC. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I do not need to accept their latest 0.76-utility offer, but their recent movement to issueA=valueA and issueC=valueA suggests a counter that preserves those concessions while asking them to move on issueB is reasonable. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional cheap talk, so I am relying on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.844 by holding firm on high-value issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE while making a meaningful concession on issueC and accepting the opponent’s repeated preference for issueF: valueC. At t=0.36 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest bid has moved close to my preferred terms except issueC, so offering issueC: valueB is a reasonable midpoint rather than conceding all the way to valueC.

I’m targeting a high-utility bid for me around 0.92 while making a real concession on issueF, where I have lower weight and the opponent has consistently insisted on valueC. At t=0.38 there is still time, so I should not over-concede, but the opponent’s recent offers show movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, with issueE already aligned. Their messages are cooperative but cheap talk, so I’m relying mainly on the offer pattern.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility: a meaningful concession from my maximum while still protecting the high-weight issues, especially issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.40 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest 0.824-utility offer outright; their bids show real movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD despite the emotional messaging. I will concede issueA to valueB and keep issueF at their consistently requested valueC, while asking them to meet me on issueC: valueA.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.824 by conceding on lower-cost or already-signaled issues like issueF and issueC while holding issueB at my top value and avoiding issueD=valueC. At t=0.42 there is some time pressure to move from my earlier near-maximal bids, and the opponent’s recent offers show real movement on issueB and occasional movement on issueA/issueC despite their emotional messaging. This bid uses only valid issues and values and is a meaningful concession from my prior position without dropping too far.

I am targeting a utility a little above 0.82, which is an appropriate mid-game concession while still protecting the higher-weight issues. The opponent’s offers have improved on issueB, issueC, and issueD, while they consistently hold issueE at valueA and issueF at valueC; their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional cheap talk, so I rely on the bidding pattern. This bid keeps issueB and issueC at values they have recently signaled, concedes issueF, and asks for a moderate compromise on issueA rather than their current valueC.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.78 by holding firm on high-value issueB and keeping issueC at the moderate valueB, while conceding on lower-weight issueE and issueF where the opponent has repeatedly signaled resistance. At t=0.46 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their latest low-utility offer; their bids show concessions on issueA/issueB/issueC at times, while their emotional messages are cheap talk and do not override the numerical pattern.

At t=0.48 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a solid but more acceptable utility around 0.80 rather than holding only maximum bids. The opponent’s recent offers show movement on issueB, issueC, and issueD while consistently pushing issueF: valueC, so I concede issueF and issueC while holding a better position on issueA and keeping high-value issueD/issueE.

At t=0.50 I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.84 while making a focused concession on issueF, which the opponent has consistently kept at valueC. The opponent’s latest offer moved significantly toward my preferences on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueE, but issueD at valueC is too costly for me, so I counter with the same structure except moving issueD to valueB, which is fully valuable to me and has appeared repeatedly in their prior offers.
